title: pittosporum cherrieri
intro: Pittosporum cherrieri, also known as the Australian cheesewood, is a plant species native to Australia and New Guinea. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Pittosporum cherrieri are thought to include its ability to alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, as well as its potential to support immune function and reduce inflammation. Pittosporum cherrieri is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and can also be used in tincture or capsule form, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and potential side effects.
